title: MathNet: a Global Multimodal Benchmark for Mathematical Reasoning and Retrieval
method: Constructing a dataset of 30,676 expert-authored math problems with solutions across 47 countries, 17 languages, and two decades of competitions, along with a retrieval benchmark of equivalent and structurally similar...
scenario: MathNet dataset with Olympiad-level math problems from 47 countries, 17 languages, and two decades of competitions. Retrieval benchmark with mathematically equivalent and structurally similar problem pairs.
advantages: Introducing MathNet, a high-quality, large-scale, multimodal, and multilingual dataset of Olympiad-level math problems with a benchmark for evaluating mathematical reasoning and retrieval.
limitations: Not explicitly mentioned in the abstract

title: MASS-RAG: Multi-Agent Synthesis Retrieval-Augmented Generation
method: Distinct agents for evidence summarization, evidence extraction, and reasoning over retrieved documents, with a dedicated synthesis stage to combine their outputs.
advantages: A multi-agent synthesis approach to RAG that structures evidence processing into multiple role-specialized agents.

method: WorldDB, a memory engine with recursive worlds (containers with subgraphs), content-addressed immutable nodes, and typed edges with behavioral handlers.
